meta.title: React Hooks
meta.description: Implement each hook from scratch, then verify it with the demo component.
meta.instructions: Implement the hook stub in App.tsx. The Demo component is already wired up — make it work.

challenges.title: useToggle
challenges.prompt: Implement a `useToggle(initialValue?: boolean)` hook that returns `[value, toggle]`. `toggle` should accept an optional explicit boolean — if passed, set to that value; otherwise flip.
required: Returns [value: boolean, toggle: (next?: boolean) => void]
Calling toggle() with no argument flips the value
Calling toggle(true) or toggle(false) sets explicitly
Initial value defaults to false
bonus: TypeScript generic so it can toggle any two values (e.g. 'light' | 'dark')
reset() function as third return value
challenges.demo: Render a button that shows 'ON' or 'OFF' and toggles on click. Add a second button that forces it OFF.

challenges.title: useCounter
challenges.prompt: Implement `useCounter(initialValue?: number, options?: { min?: number; max?: number; step?: number })` that returns `{ count, increment, decrement, reset, set }`.
required: increment and decrement respect min/max bounds
reset returns to initial value
set clamps to min/max
step defaults to 1
bonus: incrementBy(n) and decrementBy(n) helpers
isMin and isMax booleans in the return value
challenges.demo: Render a counter display with +/- buttons, a Reset button, and min=0 / max=10 bounds. Show disabled state at limits.

challenges.title: usePrevious
challenges.prompt: Implement `usePrevious<T>(value: T): T | undefined` that returns the value from the previous render.
required: Returns undefined on the first render
Returns the value before the most recent change
Works with any type (number, string, object)
bonus: usePreviousDistinct — only updates when the value actually changes (deep or shallow compare)
challenges.demo: Render an input. Show 'current: X' and 'previous: Y' below it, updating live as the user types.

challenges.title: useDebounce
challenges.prompt: Implement `useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay: number): T` that returns the debounced version of a value, updated only after the delay has passed without changes.
required: Returns the original value immediately on mount
Delays updating the returned value by `delay` ms after the input changes
Clears the timer on cleanup to avoid stale updates
bonus: leading option: fire immediately on first change, then debounce subsequent ones
maxWait option: force an update if value keeps changing beyond this duration
challenges.demo: Render a search input. Show 'Live: X' (instant) and 'Debounced: Y' (300ms delay). Count how many times the debounced value updated.

challenges.title: useThrottle
challenges.prompt: Implement `useThrottle<T>(value: T, interval: number): T` that throttles a value to update at most once per interval.
required: Returns the value immediately on first render
Subsequent updates are throttled to at most once per interval ms
Cleans up the timer on unmount
bonus: trailing option: emit the last value after the interval even if no new update arrives
challenges.demo: Render a mouse-move tracker (mousemove on a div). Show 'Live: x,y' and 'Throttled: x,y' (200ms). Show how many times each updated.

challenges.title: useLocalStorage
challenges.prompt: Implement `useLocalStorage<T>(key: string, initialValue: T): [T, (value: T | ((prev: T) => T)) => void, () => void]` that syncs state with localStorage.
required: Reads the initial value from localStorage on mount (parsing JSON)
Writes to localStorage on every state update
Returns a remove() function that deletes the key and resets to initialValue
Handles JSON parse errors gracefully (falls back to initialValue)
bonus: Syncs across tabs via the 'storage' event
Accepts a custom serializer/deserializer pair
challenges.demo: Render a text input whose value persists across page refreshes. Show a 'Clear' button. Open two tabs to verify sync.

challenges.title: useSessionStorage
challenges.prompt: Implement `useSessionStorage<T>(key: string, initialValue: T)` — identical to useLocalStorage but backed by sessionStorage.
required: Reads from sessionStorage on mount
Writes on state update
Falls back to initialValue on parse error
Returns [value, setValue, remove]
bonus: Share implementation with useLocalStorage via a useStorage(storage, key, initial) abstraction
challenges.demo: Same as useLocalStorage demo but note that clearing the session (closing the tab) resets it.

challenges.title: useEventListener
challenges.prompt: Implement `useEventListener<K extends keyof WindowEventMap>(eventName: K, handler: (event: WindowEventMap[K]) => void, element?: React.RefObject<Element | null> | Window | null, options?: AddEventListenerOptions)` that attaches and cleans up event listeners.
required: Defaults to window if no element ref is passed
Attaches listener on mount, removes on unmount
Re-attaches if eventName or element changes
Uses a ref for the handler so it's always current without re-subscribing
bonus: Overload that accepts document or custom EventTarget
Passive option for scroll/touch events
challenges.demo: Track keydown events globally and show the last pressed key. Also track clicks on a specific box element.

challenges.title: useKeyPress
challenges.prompt: Implement `useKeyPress(targetKey: string): boolean` that returns true while the specified key is held down.
required: Returns true on keydown, false on keyup
Resets to false when the component unmounts
Matches against event.key
bonus: Accept an array of keys, return true if any is pressed
Accept modifier options: { ctrl, shift, alt }
challenges.demo: Render a display that shows which of the arrow keys are currently held. Each key lights up independently.

challenges.title: useHover
challenges.prompt: Implement `useHover<T extends Element>(): [React.RefObject<T>, boolean]` that returns a ref to attach to an element and a boolean indicating hover state.
required: Returns [ref, isHovered]
isHovered is true between mouseenter and mouseleave
Cleans up listeners on unmount
bonus: Delay option: hover must persist for N ms before isHovered becomes true
challenges.demo: Render a card that changes background when hovered. Show 'hovered' / 'not hovered' text below.

challenges.title: useClickAway
challenges.prompt: Implement `useClickAway<T extends Element>(handler: (event: MouseEvent | TouchEvent) => void): React.RefObject<T>` that calls handler when a click occurs outside the referenced element.
required: Fires handler on mousedown outside the ref element
Also handles touchstart for mobile
Does not fire if the ref element itself is clicked
Cleans up listeners on unmount
bonus: Accept multiple refs
Option to disable the listener
challenges.demo: Render a dropdown that closes when you click outside it. The dropdown opens on button click.

challenges.title: useMediaQuery
challenges.prompt: Implement `useMediaQuery(query: string): boolean` that returns whether a CSS media query currently matches.
required: Returns true/false based on the current match
Updates reactively when the viewport changes
Cleans up the listener on unmount or query change
bonus: SSR-safe: return false (or a provided initialValue) during server-side rendering
challenges.demo: Render different layouts for mobile (<768px) and desktop. Show the current query result as text.

challenges.title: useIntersectionObserver
challenges.prompt: Implement `useIntersectionObserver(options?: IntersectionObserverInit): [React.RefObject<Element>, IntersectionObserverEntry | null]` that tracks element visibility.
required: Returns [ref, entry] — attach ref to an element to observe it
entry is null until the first intersection event
Disconnects the observer on unmount
Passes options (threshold, root, rootMargin) to IntersectionObserver
bonus: Once option: disconnect after first intersection
freezeOnceVisible: stop updating after element first becomes visible
challenges.demo: Render a long scrollable list. The last item has the ref attached. Show 'Last item visible: yes/no' in a fixed header.

challenges.title: useInterval
challenges.prompt: Implement `useInterval(callback: () => void, delay: number | null)` that runs a callback on a repeating interval. Passing null pauses it.
required: Starts the interval on mount
Pauses when delay is null
Restarts if delay changes
Always uses the latest callback without restarting the interval
bonus: Return a stop/start/reset API
challenges.demo: Render a live clock (HH:MM:SS). Add Pause/Resume buttons that pass null or a delay.

challenges.title: useIntervalWhen
challenges.prompt: Implement `useIntervalWhen(callback: () => void, options: { ms: number; when: boolean; startImmediately?: boolean })` — like useInterval but controlled by a boolean.
required: Only runs the interval while `when` is true
startImmediately fires the callback once before the first interval tick when when becomes true
Cleans up on unmount
bonus: Expose the count of how many times the callback has fired
challenges.demo: Render an auto-incrementing counter that only ticks while a 'Running' toggle is on.

challenges.title: useTimeout
challenges.prompt: Implement `useTimeout(callback: () => void, delay: number | null): { reset: () => void; clear: () => void }` that fires a callback once after a delay.
required: Fires callback once after delay ms
reset() restarts the timer
clear() cancels the timer
Passing null cancels immediately
Always uses the latest callback
bonus: Return isExpired: boolean
challenges.demo: Render a 'Self-destructing message' that disappears after 5 seconds. Add Reset and Cancel buttons.

challenges.title: useCountdown
challenges.prompt: Implement `useCountdown(targetDate: Date | number): [number, { start: () => void; stop: () => void; reset: () => void }]` that returns seconds remaining and controls.
required: Returns seconds remaining (0 when expired)
Updates every second
stop() pauses, start() resumes, reset() restores to original target
Stops automatically at 0
bonus: Return { days, hours, minutes, seconds } object instead of raw seconds
onComplete callback option
challenges.demo: Render a countdown timer to a configurable future date. Show days/hours/minutes/seconds. Add Start/Stop/Reset buttons.

challenges.title: useRandomInterval
challenges.prompt: Implement `useRandomInterval(callback: () => void, minDelay: number, maxDelay: number)` that fires a callback at random intervals between min and max ms.
required: Each interval picks a new random delay between minDelay and maxDelay
Cleans up on unmount
Always uses latest callback
bonus: Return { stop, start } controls
active option to pause
challenges.demo: Render confetti dots that appear at random positions at random intervals (200–800ms).

challenges.title: useWindowSize
challenges.prompt: Implement `useWindowSize(): { width: number; height: number }` that returns the current window dimensions and updates on resize.
required: Returns { width, height } from window.innerWidth / innerHeight
Updates on window resize
Debounces or throttles the resize handler
Cleans up the listener on unmount
bonus: SSR-safe defaults (0 or undefined when window is not available)
challenges.demo: Render a live display of window width × height. Resize the browser to verify it updates.

challenges.title: useWindowScroll
challenges.prompt: Implement `useWindowScroll(): [{ x: number; y: number }, (pos: { x: number; y: number }) => void]` that returns scroll position and a scroll-to function.
required: Returns current window.scrollX and scrollY
Updates on scroll
The setter calls window.scrollTo with the given coordinates
Cleans up scroll listener on unmount
bonus: smooth option that uses behavior: 'smooth' in scrollTo
challenges.demo: Render a long page (>2000px). Show a fixed 'scroll: x,y' indicator. Add a 'Back to top' button.

challenges.title: useDocumentTitle
challenges.prompt: Implement `useDocumentTitle(title: string, options?: { restoreOnUnmount?: boolean })` that sets document.title.
required: Sets document.title to the provided string
Updates when the title prop changes
With restoreOnUnmount: true, restores the previous title on unmount
bonus: Format option: (title: string) => string to add a suffix like ' | My App'
challenges.demo: Render a multi-step form. Each step sets a different document title. Show the title in a status bar on the page too.

challenges.title: useFavicon
challenges.prompt: Implement `useFavicon(url: string)` that dynamically updates the page favicon.
required: Creates or updates the <link rel='icon'> element in the document head
Updates when the url prop changes
Does not create duplicate link elements
bonus: Support emoji favicon: accept a string emoji and render it to a canvas
challenges.demo: Render buttons for different favicon URLs (or emoji). Click each to change the favicon.

challenges.title: useCopyToClipboard
challenges.prompt: Implement `useCopyToClipboard(): [{ value: string | null; error: Error | null }, (text: string) => Promise<void>]` using the Clipboard API.
required: Uses navigator.clipboard.writeText
Returns the last copied value and any error
Handles browsers that don't support the Clipboard API gracefully
bonus: Auto-reset state after N ms
Fallback using document.execCommand for older browsers
challenges.demo: Render a list of code snippets. Each has a 'Copy' button that shows a '✓ Copied!' checkmark for 2 seconds.

challenges.title: useGeolocation
challenges.prompt: Implement `useGeolocation(options?: PositionOptions): { loading: boolean; latitude: number | null; longitude: number | null; accuracy: number | null; error: GeolocationPositionError | null }` that requests and tracks the device's location.
required: Returns loading: true while waiting for first position
Populates latitude, longitude, accuracy from GeolocationPosition
Populates error if permission denied or unavailable
Calls watchPosition to track movement
Clears the watch on unmount
bonus: Return altitude, speed, heading from the position coords
challenges.demo: Render a geolocation card showing coordinates (or error). Add a 'Request Location' button that triggers on demand.

challenges.title: useBattery
challenges.prompt: Implement `useBattery(): { supported: boolean; loading: boolean; level: number | null; charging: boolean | null; chargingTime: number | null; dischargingTime: number | null }` using the Battery Status API.
required: Checks navigator.getBattery support
Returns loading: true until the battery info resolves
Subscribes to levelchange, chargingchange events
Unsubscribes on unmount
bonus: Show a visual battery indicator bar
challenges.demo: Render a battery status card. Show level as a percentage and a charging indicator.

challenges.title: useNetworkState
challenges.prompt: Implement `useNetworkState(): { online: boolean; downlink?: number; effectiveType?: string; rtt?: number; saveData?: boolean }` using the Network Information API.
required: Returns online from navigator.onLine
Subscribes to online/offline events
Reads connection info from navigator.connection if available
Cleans up on unmount
bonus: Return type: '4g' | '3g' | '2g' | 'slow-2g' | null
challenges.demo: Render a network status badge (green/red for online/offline). Toggle airplane mode to test.

challenges.title: useVisibilityChange
challenges.prompt: Implement `useVisibilityChange(): boolean` that returns true when the page is visible and false when hidden.
required: Uses the Page Visibility API (document.visibilityState)
Updates on visibilitychange event
Cleans up listener on unmount
bonus: Track total time the page was visible vs hidden
challenges.demo: Render a counter that only increments while the page is visible. Switch tabs to pause it.

challenges.title: useIdle
challenges.prompt: Implement `useIdle(timeout: number): boolean` that returns true after the user has been idle (no mouse/key/touch events) for the specified duration.
required: Tracks mousemove, keydown, touchstart events to detect activity
Returns true after timeout ms of inactivity
Resets to false on any activity
Cleans up listeners on unmount
bonus: Accept a custom events list
Return the timestamp of last activity
challenges.demo: Render a status indicator: 'Active' (green) or 'Idle' (yellow) after 3 seconds of no input.

challenges.title: usePageLeave
challenges.prompt: Implement `usePageLeave(onPageLeave: () => void)` that calls the callback when the user's cursor leaves the browser window.
required: Fires onPageLeave on the mouseleave event on document
Always uses the latest callback without re-subscribing
Cleans up on unmount
bonus: Also fire on visibilitychange to hidden
challenges.demo: Render a 'Come back!' message counter that increments each time the cursor leaves the page.

challenges.title: useLockBodyScroll
challenges.prompt: Implement `useLockBodyScroll()` that prevents the document body from scrolling while the component is mounted.
required: Sets overflow: hidden on document.body on mount
Restores the previous overflow value on unmount
Works correctly if multiple components call it simultaneously
bonus: Accept a boolean active prop so the lock can be toggled
challenges.demo: Render a modal with a button. Opening the modal locks body scroll. Closing restores it.

challenges.title: useMeasure
challenges.prompt: Implement `useMeasure<T extends Element>(): [React.RefObject<T>, { width: number; height: number; top: number; left: number }]` that measures an element's bounding box using ResizeObserver.
required: Returns [ref, bounds]
bounds updates whenever the element resizes
Disconnects the ResizeObserver on unmount
Initial bounds are all 0 until first measurement
bonus: Also return x, y, right, bottom from DOMRect
challenges.demo: Render a resizable textarea (resize: both). Show width × height updating live as the user drags the resize handle.

challenges.title: useMouse
challenges.prompt: Implement `useMouse(): { x: number; y: number; elementX: number; elementY: number; elementPositionX: number; elementPositionY: number; ref: React.RefObject<Element> }` that tracks global mouse position and position relative to a ref element.
required: Tracks window-relative x, y on mousemove
elementX, elementY are mouse position relative to the ref element's top-left
elementPositionX, elementPositionY are the element's position in the viewport
Cleans up the listener
bonus: Track whether mouse is currently inside the element (isOver)
challenges.demo: Render a tracking box. Show global x,y and element-relative x,y as the mouse moves inside and outside the box.

challenges.title: useLongPress
challenges.prompt: Implement `useLongPress(callback: (e: MouseEvent | TouchEvent) => void, options?: { delay?: number; onStart?: () => void; onFinish?: () => void; onCancel?: () => void })` that fires after a sustained press.
required: Fires callback after delay ms of continuous mousedown/touchstart
Cancels if mouse/touch is released before delay (calls onCancel)
Works for both mouse and touch events
Cleans up timers on unmount
bonus: Prevent default context menu on long press
challenges.demo: Render a button that shows 'Tap' on click and 'Long pressed!' after a 500ms hold.

challenges.title: useScript
challenges.prompt: Implement `useScript(src: string): 'idle' | 'loading' | 'ready' | 'error'` that dynamically loads a script tag and tracks its status.
required: Appends a <script> tag to document.body if not already present
Returns 'loading' while loading, 'ready' on load, 'error' on error
Does not add duplicate script tags if the same src is already present
Cleans up event listeners (but does not remove the script tag)
bonus: accept a boolean removeOnUnmount to delete the script on cleanup
challenges.demo: Load the Lodash CDN script. Show the loading status. Once 'ready', render a button that calls _.shuffle([1,2,3]).

challenges.title: useFetch
challenges.prompt: Implement `useFetch<T>(url: string | null): { data: T | null; loading: boolean; error: Error | null }` that fetches JSON and handles loading/error states.
required: Fetches on mount and when url changes
Does not fetch if url is null
Prevents stale responses: ignores results from outdated fetches (abortController or ignore flag)
Returns loading: true while in-flight
bonus: Cache results by URL (Map)
refetch() function to manually retry
challenges.demo: Render a user card that fetches from https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/1. Show a skeleton while loading and an error state on failure.

challenges.title: useContinuousRetry
challenges.prompt: Implement `useContinuousRetry(callback: () => boolean, interval?: number): boolean` that keeps retrying a callback until it returns true.
required: Calls callback on mount, then every interval ms until it returns true
Returns true once the callback succeeds, false otherwise
Clears the interval on success or unmount
Default interval is 100ms
bonus: maxRetries option: stop and return false after N attempts
Expose retryCount
challenges.demo: Simulate a resource that becomes available after 2 seconds (set a timeout, flip a ref). Show loading spinner until useContinuousRetry returns true.

challenges.title: useHistoryState
challenges.prompt: Implement `useHistoryState<T>(initialState: T): { state: T; set: (s: T) => void; undo: () => void; redo: () => void; canUndo: boolean; canRedo: boolean; history: T[]; pointer: number }` that adds undo/redo to any state.
required: set() pushes a new entry onto the history stack
undo() moves the pointer back; redo() moves it forward
canUndo and canRedo reflect whether moves are possible
New set() after undo discards the redo stack
bonus: maxHistory option to cap stack size
clear() to reset to initial state
challenges.demo: Render a drawing canvas (SVG lines). Each stroke is a state entry. Show Undo/Redo buttons with disabled state.

challenges.title: useObjectState
challenges.prompt: Implement `useObjectState<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(initialState: T): [T, (patch: Partial<T> | ((prev: T) => Partial<T>)) => void]` that merges partial updates into an object state.
required: Merges partial updates shallowly (like this.setState in class components)
Accepts a function updater
Does not mutate nested objects
bonus: reset() function to restore to initialState
TypeScript: returned setter is strongly typed to Partial<T>
challenges.demo: Render a user profile form (name, email, age). Each field updates independently with a single setState call.

challenges.title: useDefault
challenges.prompt: Implement `useDefault<T>(initialValue: T, defaultValue: T): [T, React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<T | null | undefined>>]` that returns defaultValue when state is null or undefined.
required: Wraps useState
Returns defaultValue when the state is null or undefined
Returns the actual state value otherwise
Setter accepts null/undefined to trigger the default
bonus: Accept a custom isEmpty predicate instead of null/undefined check
challenges.demo: Render an input bound to useDefault('', '(empty)'). Show how setting it to null/undefined renders the default value.

challenges.title: useList
challenges.prompt: Implement `useList<T>(initialList: T[]): [T[], { set: (list: T[]) => void; push: (...items: T[]) => void; removeAt: (index: number) => void; insertAt: (index: number, item: T) => void; updateAt: (index: number, item: T) => void; clear: () => void }]`.
required: push appends items
removeAt removes by index
insertAt inserts without mutation
updateAt replaces the item at an index
clear empties the list
bonus: filter(predicate) helper
sort(compareFn) helper
challenges.demo: Render a todo list. Add items with an input, remove with an X button, reorder with up/down buttons.

challenges.title: useMap
challenges.prompt: Implement `useMap<K, V>(initialMap?: Map<K, V> | [K, V][]): [Map<K, V>, { set: (key: K, value: V) => void; delete: (key: K) => void; clear: () => void; reset: () => void }]`.
required: Wraps a Map in state, returning a stable actions object
set and delete create a new Map (immutable update)
reset restores the initial map
clear empties the map
bonus: has(key) and get(key) helpers in the actions object
challenges.demo: Render a key-value store editor: add entries with a key+value form, delete individual entries, show all entries in a table.

challenges.title: useSet
challenges.prompt: Implement `useSet<T>(initialSet?: Set<T> | T[]): [Set<T>, { add: (item: T) => void; delete: (item: T) => void; toggle: (item: T) => void; clear: () => void; reset: () => void }]`.
required: add and delete create a new Set (immutable update)
toggle adds if not present, removes if present
reset restores the initial set
clear empties the set
bonus: has(item) helper
challenges.demo: Render a multi-select tag picker. Clicking a tag toggles selection. Show selected tags in a summary.

challenges.title: useQueue
challenges.prompt: Implement `useQueue<T>(initialQueue?: T[]): { queue: T[]; add: (item: T) => void; remove: () => T | undefined; clear: () => void; first: T | undefined; last: T | undefined; size: number }` — a FIFO queue.
required: add enqueues to the end
remove dequeues from the front and returns the item
first and last are the front and back elements
size is the current queue length
bonus: peek() without removing
isEmpty boolean
challenges.demo: Render a message queue: add messages with an input, process (remove) the oldest one with a button. Show front/back labels.

challenges.title: useIsClient
challenges.prompt: Implement `useIsClient(): boolean` that returns false during SSR and true after hydration on the client.
required: Returns false on the initial render (compatible with SSR)
Returns true after the component has mounted on the client
Uses useEffect or useState initialiser to detect client
bonus: useIsomorphicLayoutEffect — use useLayoutEffect on client, useEffect on server
challenges.demo: Render a component that shows window.innerWidth. Use useIsClient to guard against SSR crashes.

challenges.title: useIsFirstRender
challenges.prompt: Implement `useIsFirstRender(): boolean` that returns true only on the first render.
required: Returns true on the very first render
Returns false on all subsequent renders
Uses a ref (not state) to avoid triggering re-renders
bonus: useUpdateEffect — like useEffect but skips the first render
challenges.demo: Render a form that shows 'Initial render' on mount and 'Updated' on every re-render. Use the hook to differentiate.

challenges.title: useRenderCount
challenges.prompt: Implement `useRenderCount(): number` that returns how many times the component has rendered.
required: Returns 1 on the first render, 2 on the second, etc.
Uses a ref so the counter itself doesn't cause re-renders
Works correctly in React StrictMode (double-invocation)
bonus: useWhyDidYouRender — log which prop or state changed to cause the re-render
challenges.demo: Render a component with several pieces of state. Show the render count. Add buttons that change state to trigger re-renders.

challenges.title: useRenderInfo
challenges.prompt: Implement `useRenderInfo(name?: string): { name: string; renders: number; sinceLastRender: number; timestamp: number }` that exposes debug render timing.
required: renders: how many times the component has rendered
sinceLastRender: ms since the previous render
timestamp: Date.now() of the current render
Logs render info to console in development
bonus: Accept a filter so it only logs when specific values change
challenges.demo: Render a component that re-renders on a 1s interval. Show the render info table. Verify sinceLastRender is ~1000ms.

challenges.title: useLogger
challenges.prompt: Implement `useLogger(componentName: string, ...props: unknown[])` that logs mount, update, and unmount lifecycle events to the console.
required: Logs '[ComponentName] mounted' with props on mount
Logs '[ComponentName] updated' with new props on each update
Logs '[ComponentName] unmounted' on cleanup
Only logs in development (import.meta.env.DEV or NODE_ENV)
bonus: Accept a custom logger function
challenges.demo: Render a component with a counter prop. Increment the counter. Observe mount, update, and unmount logs in the console.

challenges.title: usePreferredLanguage
challenges.prompt: Implement `usePreferredLanguage(): string` that returns the user's preferred language from navigator.language and updates if it changes.
required: Returns navigator.language on mount
Listens for languagechange on window
Cleans up the listener on unmount
Returns 'en' as a fallback if navigator.language is undefined
bonus: Return navigator.languages (full array of preferred languages)
challenges.demo: Render the preferred language and a greeting translated into that language (English, Spanish, French, German, Japanese).

challenges.title: useOrientation
challenges.prompt: Implement `useOrientation(): { angle: number; type: 'landscape-primary' | 'landscape-secondary' | 'portrait-primary' | 'portrait-secondary' }` using the Screen Orientation API.
required: Returns current screen.orientation.angle and type
Updates on the orientationchange event
Cleans up listener on unmount
Falls back to window.innerWidth > window.innerHeight check if Screen Orientation API is unavailable
bonus: Return a simplified isPortrait / isLandscape boolean
challenges.demo: Render an orientation indicator that rotates a device icon to match the current orientation. Test on a mobile device or DevTools.
